title: OREGON TRIBAL STUDENT GRANT - 211info
text: HIGHER EDUCATION COORDINATING COMMISSION OREGON TRIBAL STUDENT GRANT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides funding to offset the cost of attendance at colleges and universities. 3225 25th Street SE Salem, OR 97302 Eligibility: -Must be an enrolled member of one of the nine federally recognized tribes in Oregon -Must be enrolled or accepted for enrollment as a student at an Oregon college or university -Must be enrolled at least half-time -Priority for Undergraduate and Master students -Applicants who have already earned a bachelor's degree are not eligible for the grant if they are working towards an associate's degree, a second bachelor's degree, or an undergraduate certificate. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 687-7400 2 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 687-7400 ext 2 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website, call, or email for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake AREA SERVED: State of Oregon Services: Student Financial Aid * Native American Community Other Services Offered At This Location: EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - CHAFEE EDUCATION AND TRAINING VOUCHER PROGRAM EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - DECEASED OR DISABLED PUBLIC SAFETY OFFICER GRANT EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - OFFICE OF STUDENT ACCESS AND COMPLETION EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON BARBER AND HAIRDRESSER GRANT PROGRAM EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON OPPORTUNITY GRANT EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON PROMISE GRANT EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON TEACHER SCHOLARS PROGRAM GRANT MILITARY SERVICE - OREGON NATIONAL GUARD STATE TUITION ASSISTANCE PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - OREGON STUDENT CHILD CARE GRANT If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. HIGHER EDUCATION COORDINATING COMMISSION
